data.title: The Hartford Insurance Gr Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
data.description:
The Hartford Insurance Gr (NYSE:HIG) reported first-quarter financial results on Friday. The transcript from the company's first-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/517599819

Summary

The Hartford Insurance Gr reported strong Q1 2026 results with core earnings of $866 million and a core earnings ROE of 20.3%.

Business Insurance saw a 6% written premium growth with an underlying combined ratio of 89.2, highlighting underwriting discipline and strategic pricing.

The company continues to focus on expanding its small business ecosystem and leveraging technology to enhance underwriting processes.

In Personal Insurance, the underlying combined ratio improved, though growth was impacted by a competitive market; new product rollouts in the agency channel are progressing well.

Employee Benefits reported a core earnings margin of 6.9%, driven by strong life and disability performance and increased sales activity.

Investment income remains robust, supported by a diversified portfolio with a focus on maintaining strong credit quality.

Management emphasized maintaining discipline in pricing and underwriting amidst competitive pressures and technological advancements.

The company plans to maintain its share repurchase program, with $1.1 billion remaining authorized through December 2026.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Kate Jordan

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Beth Costello (Chief Financial Officer)

OPERATOR

As a reminder to ask a question, please press star followed by the number one on your telephone keypad. In the interest of time, we ask that you please limit yourselves to one question and one follow up question. Thank you. Our first question comes from Andrew Kliegerman from TD Cowan. Please go ahead. Your line is open.

Andrew Kliegerman (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mo

Andrew Kliegerman (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mo

You mentioned it quickly, but I think what we're finding, Andrew, is that all of the capabilities We've built over 30 years in small really matter a lot in middle. And those same capabilities we believe can help us grow profitably in the middle space because the agents, there's a margin pressure they're feeling as well and as much help as we can give them helps grow their margins in the middle market space as well.

Andrew Kliegerman (Equity Analyst)

Thanks Chris and Mo, appreciate it.

OPERATOR

Elise Greenspan (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Elise Greenspan (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Maybe just one point at least. The additional piece I would give you is the flows continue to be really strong. That is submissions into both the retail that is the admitted part of our small business franchise and the non admitted and that flow and our hit rates are relatively flat. So we feel really good about this. You know, the continued flow that we're getting from our agents, which demonstrates to us the strength and position we have with them.

OPERATOR

Thank you. Our next question comes from Brian Meredith from ubs. Please go ahead. Your line is open.

Brian Meredith (Equity Analyst)

Yeah, thank you. First of all, Chris, you've got some good ENS capabilities. Maybe you can talk a little bit about what you're seeing in the market as far as is business kind of flowing back to the standard markets from the ENS markets at this point in the cycle. Are we kind of heading in that position then maybe also talk a little bit about what you're seeing with MGA's competitive competition out there.

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

You know, Brian, I'm going to let Mo answer that just because he's the principal architect of a lot of our growth strategies, you know, in the marketplace. But I would just say I feel a level of stability and I don't sense a lot of movement one way or the other. But Mo, what would you really say?

Mo

Brian Meredith (Equity Analyst)

Great, thanks. And then second question. I was hoping to chat a little bit about workers comp. You know, kind of where are we with respect to. I mean, it's still a competitive market out there. I know you're kind of expecting some margin deterioration this year in comparison. Where are we as far as profitability? Are we getting to a point? Maybe we can see some leveling out in comp and maybe some improvement. No pricing.

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mo

Brian Meredith (Equity Analyst)

Great. Thank you.

OPERATOR

Our next question comes from Mike Zaremski from BMO Capital Markets. Please go ahead. Your line is open.

Mike Zaremski (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mike Zaremski (Equity Analyst)

No, you did. We just get a lot of questions from investors about that, seem a bit worried about the pricing cycle. And I think we're trying to remind folks that this guy's not falling. So I'm just kind of pivoting just for specifically to global specialty. Since you called pricing being up a bit. Just curious what caused that, if it's worth talking about. Thanks.

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Yeah, I would say moen, you can add your color. I would say wholesale, particularly on the primary liability side and a little excess casualty where the team is really being disciplined and focused on getting rate. As I said with anything with liability in the product line, we are super focused on getting the needed rate. But mo any color, I think that's the key point. I think the rest of the book is fairly stable.

Mo

Mike, if you think financial lines is it is where it has been. We didn't feel dramatic change Marines the same. We have a lot of lines within that global specialty book, but I think the standout in the quarter was the rate coming back up in wholesale casualty lines.

Mike Zaremski (Equity Analyst)

Thank you.

OPERATOR

Our next question comes from David Matamatin from Evercore isi. Please go Ahead, your line is open.

David Matamatin (Equity Analyst)

Hey, thanks. Good morning. I was hoping you guys could unpack some of the movement in the underlying loss ratio in business insurance this quarter and maybe just talk a little bit about how much of a headwind workers comp is within there and some of the other moving pieces and maybe just given the pricing environment, how you're thinking about that for the rest of the year.

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Beth Costello (Chief Financial Officer)

David Matamatin (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mike Fish

David Matamatin (Equity Analyst)

Great, thanks for the color there.

OPERATOR

Our next question comes from Katie Sakis from from Autonomous Research. Please go ahead. Your line is open. Good morning. I wanted to shift back to the BI reserve for a moment. I think, you know, excluding the legacy charge this quarter, the core general liability book, you know, still looks quite strong with no net adverse development for several quarters.

Beth Costello (Chief Financial Officer)

Katie Sakis (Equity Analyst)

Okay. And then, Beth, you mentioned in prepared remarks that direct lending and BDC exposure is about 2% of invested assets. Can you help us understand how much of that exposure is to software or adjacent borrowers?

Beth Costello (Chief Financial Officer)

OPERATOR

Got it. Thank you. Our next question comes from Gregory Peters. From Raymond James, please go ahead. Your line is open.

Gregory Peters (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mike Fish

Gregory Peters (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mo

Gregory Peters (Equity Analyst)

Fair enough. Thanks for the answers.

OPERATOR

Our next question comes from Rob Cox from Goldman Sachs. Please go ahead. Your line is open.

Rob Cox (Equity Analyst)

Beth Costello (Chief Financial Officer)

Rob Cox (Equity Analyst)

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mo

Rob Cox (Equity Analyst)

Thanks for the comprehensive answer.

OPERATOR

Our last question today will come from Jeroen Kinar from Mizuho Americas. Please go ahead. Your line is open.

Jeroen Kinar (Equity Analyst)

Thank you. Good morning. Just want to start with personal auto. You know just given the changes in the in the competitive environment today, do you expect that to continue now with the straight up hormone situation? And you know on the one hand maybe you have better frequency coming out with lower gasoline prices. On the other hand you have maybe supply chain issues driving severity up. So do you see any impact on the competitive environment with all that?

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Jeroen Kinar (Equity Analyst)

Thank you, that's helpful. And then maybe circling back to the last line of questions around AI and the intermediaries. Is there a risk that the Hartford's negotiating power with intermediaries in small commercial would diminish if larger brokers are able to use AI to move down market and infringe on space that's really been dominated by smaller agents?

Chris Swift (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

Mo

Jeroen Kinar (Equity Analyst)

But I understand that you may see more flow, but ultimately don't they have stronger negotiating position when they try to determine the terms of the actual contract or policy?

Mo

Yeah, I think this is where the balance of power is actually really equal. Because of all the capabilities we bring. There's very few people who can bring what we bring in the small business space. And I can prove to any agent and broker how we can make a penny or two more in every dollar for them. That leads to some really productive conversations.

Jeroen Kinar (Equity Analyst)

Makes sense. Thank you so much.

OPERATOR

And we are out of time for questions. I would like to turn the call back to Kate Jordan for any closing remarks.

Kate Jordan

Thanks for joining us today. As always, feel free to follow up with any additional questions and have a great day.

data.title: What's Going On With AMC Stock Friday?
data.description:
The stock remains still tied to headline-driven risk appetite even with "The Super Mario Galaxy Movie" holding at $628.7 million worldwide after two weekends.

  • AMC Entertainment stock is under selling pressure. What’s pulling AMC shares down?

What’s Driving AMC Stock Friday?

AMC Entertainment is still trading the same two levers: whether a 45-day theatrical-only window can stabilize admissions, and whether consolidation talk can translate into a steadier release cadence. CEO Adam Aron has framed the 45-day window as the key economic lever as Paramount Skydance–Warner Bros. Discovery tie-up chatter continues to circulate.

The box-office datapoint traders keep anchoring to is "The Super Mario Galaxy Movie" at $628.7 million worldwide after two weekends ($300 million domestic and $320.6 million international). AMC has also flagged Mario-themed concessions as its second-best performer for popcorn buckets and related items, behind only the Taylor Swift documentary.

Critical Technical Levels for AMC Stock to Watch

AMC is rebounding off its March swing low but remains well below its $4.08 52-week high, which keeps the longer-term recovery debate unresolved. The stock is trading 17.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 9.7% above its 100-day SMA, which suggests the near-term trend is firmer than the intermediate backdrop.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, has the MACD line above the signal line with a positive histogram, which leans toward improving upside momentum after the bullish cross in May 2025. In everyday terms, MACD staying above its signal line usually means buyers are still controlling the pace, even if dips show up.

On a longer lens, the stock is down 39.85% over the last 12 months, which shows the bigger trend has stayed pressured even with periodic rallies. It's also trading 24.5% below its 200-day SMA, a reminder that the main trend hurdle still sits overhead.

  • Key Resistance: $2.00 — a round-number zone where rallies often stall first
  • Key Support: $1.50 — an area buyers have tended to defend on pullbacks

How AMC Entertainment Operates In The Movie Industry

AMC Entertainment operates movie theatres across the U.S. and Europe, and its results are tightly linked to box-office demand, release timing, and how long films stay exclusive to theaters. It also leans on higher-margin add-ons like premium formats, food-and-beverage sales, MacGuffins full bars, and AMC Dine-In locations to help offset choppy attendance.

The company reports its theatrical exhibition business across U.S. markets and International markets, with key revenue coming from the U.S. That's why the current focus on a 45-day theatrical-only window, studio consolidation chatte, and blockbuster performance can swing expectations quickly for admissions and concession attach rates.

AMC Entertainment Earnings Preview For May 2026

The countdown is on: AMC Entertainment Holdings, Inc. is set to report earnings on May 6 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 37 cents (Up from Loss of 58 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $961.39 million (Up from $862.50 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold rating with an average price target of $1.37. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Macquarie: Neutral (Lowers Target to $1.50) (Feb. 26)
  • Citigroup: Sell (Lowers Target to $1.10) (Feb. 25)
  • Roth Capital: Neutral (Lowers Target to $1.50) (Feb. 25)

AMC Stock Price Movement Overview

AMC Stock Price Activity: AMC Entertainment shares were down 3.59% at $1.61 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Trade Strategy For SPY, QQQ, AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META, And TSLA
data.description:
Good Morning Traders!

Today's economic calendar closes out the week with a lighter schedule, allowing markets to trade more on positioning and technical levels. The primary data point comes at 10:00AM ET with Final University of Michigan Sentiment, including Current Conditions, Expectations, and both short term and long term inflation expectations, which can influence consumer outlook and inflation sentiment. 

At 11:00AM ET, Kansas City Fed Services Activity provides additional insight into regional economic conditions. With limited data and the week coming to a close, expect lighter liquidity and potential positioning flows into the weekend. Markets may remain stable unless sentiment shifts from the inflation expectations component

Now, we will discuss SPY, QQQ, AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META, and TSLA.

SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY)

SPY is currently trading around 711.75 as markets close out the week holding near highs with a lighter economic calendar. If buyers defend this level early, a move toward 716.50 may develop, followed by 721.25 if momentum builds into the afternoon. Sustained strength above 726.00 would signal continued upside expansion into the weekend. 

If SPY loses 711.75 with conviction, sellers may press into 706.75. A breakdown there could expose 701.75, and continued weakness may bring the 696.75 region into focus. Expect lighter liquidity and positioning driven moves.

Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (NASDAQ:QQQ)

QQQ is currently trading around 660.50 and continues to show strong upside momentum led by tech. If buyers defend this pivot, a push toward 666.00 may develop, followed by 671.75 if momentum builds. Sustained strength above 677.00 would indicate continued leadership in growth stocks. 

If 660.50 fails to hold, sellers may drive price toward 655.50. A deeper breakdown could expose 650.50, and continued weakness may bring the 645.50 region into play. Watch semiconductors for direction.

Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL)

AAPL is currently trading around 273.25 and holding near recent highs. If buyers defend this level, price may rotate toward 277.25, followed by 281.25 if momentum builds. Sustained strength above 285.50 would indicate continued institutional demand. 

If 273.25 breaks lower, sellers may test 269.25 quickly. Continued downside pressure could extend into 265.25, and deeper weakness may bring the 261.25 region into focus.

Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ:MSFT)

MSFT is currently trading around 420.75 and consolidating after recent upside movement. If buyers defend this pivot, price may push toward 426.25, followed by 431.75 if momentum builds. A sustained move above 437.25 would signal strong continuation in large cap tech. 

If 420.75 fails to hold, sellers may press into 415.75. A deeper pullback could test 410.75, and continued weakness may bring the 405.75 region into play.

NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA)

NVDA is currently trading around 201.25 and holding above the key 200 level. If buyers defend this pivot, a move toward 207.50 may develop, followed by 213.50 if semiconductor momentum continues. Sustained trade above 219.50 would indicate strong upside expansion. 

If 201.25 fails to hold, sellers may test 196.25 quickly. Continued downside could extend into 191.25, and deeper weakness may bring the 186.25 region into focus.

Alphabet Inc Class A (NASDAQ:GOOGL)

GOOGL is currently trading around 341.00 and maintaining strong structure. If buyers defend this level, price may rotate toward 346.25, followed by 351.50 if communication services remain strong. Sustained strength above 356.50 would indicate continued upside participation. 

If 341.00 fails to hold, sellers may guide price toward 336.00. A breakdown there could expose 331.00, and continued weakness may bring the 326.00 region into play.

Meta Platforms Inc (NASDAQ:META)

META is currently trading around 662.75 and pulling back slightly after recent strength. If buyers defend this pivot, a push toward 669.75 may develop, followed by 676.75 if momentum rebuilds. Sustained strength above 683.75 would indicate continued upside leadership. 

If 662.75 breaks lower, sellers may guide price toward 656.75. A deeper pullback could test 650.75, and continued weakness may bring the 644.75 region into focus.

Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA)

TSLA is currently trading around 377.00 and attempting to stabilize after recent volatility. If buyers defend this pivot, a move toward 384.25 may develop, followed by 391.50 if speculative momentum builds. Sustained strength above 398.75 would indicate stronger upside participation. 

If 377.00 fails to hold, sellers may test 370.25 quickly. Continued downside pressure could extend into 363.50, and deeper weakness may bring the 356.75 region into play.

Final Word: Good luck, trade safely, and have a great weekend!

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Full Transcript: Eastern Bankshares Q1 2026 Earnings Call
data.description:
Eastern Bankshares (NASDAQ:EBC) reported first-quarter financial results on Friday. The transcript from the company's first-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

This transcript is brought to you by Benzinga APIs. For real-time access to our entire catalog, please visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ for a consultation.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/325174886

Summary

Eastern Bankshares reported a 31% increase in operating income and an 18% rise in operating earnings per share year-over-year, with a 12.8% return on average tangible common equity.

The company experienced a decrease in loan and deposit balances due to seasonal trends but remains optimistic due to record high commercial loan pipelines.

Eastern Bankshares completed the Harbor One merger, including the core system conversion, and is on track to achieve targeted cost savings.

The wealth management sector saw positive net flows, increasing assets under management to a record $10.3 billion.

The company anticipates completing its current share buyback program by mid-year and announced a 15% dividend increase.

Management is focusing on organic growth, share repurchases, and dividends to optimize capital and expects to manage CET1 towards the median of the KRX.

The company is leveraging AI to enhance customer engagement, offering more personalized services.

Despite competitive pressures on deposit costs, Eastern Bankshares is committed to balancing growth with maintaining a high-quality deposit base.

The company remains vigilant on asset quality, with low exposure to higher-risk sectors and a strong credit profile.

Management maintained its full-year guidance but noted potential for trending towards the lower end of the NII range due to economic uncertainties.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

OPERATOR

Patty Strickland

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Patty Strickland

Thanks, David, that's super helpful. And just one more from me. There's been some news of some larger competitors moving into the Boston market. On the wealth side, can you talk about the extent to which you, you've run into them so far, but do you expect any pressure there?

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Sure, fatty. So, yeah, there's frequently news of entrants into the market. You know, we're, we're not alone in understanding, you know, the very strong demographics

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

from a household income and wealth perspective in New England and in Massachusetts in particular. But it's, it's always been a very competitive market. We'd expect that to continue. So, so new entrants don't disturb us. We're well used to competing with others in the market and have been for many years. Dennis, I would just add to that a lot of those new entrants are focusing higher-asset amounts than, than our core bread and butter. Yeah.

Patty Strickland

All right, great. Thanks for taking my questions. I'll step back.

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Sure. Thanks, Patty.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from the line of Justin Crowley from Piper Sandler. Your line is open.

Justin Crowley

Hey, good morning, everyone.

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Morning, Justin.

Justin Crowley

Just wanted to keep on the margin. You know, you left the guide on change and you know, I think which last quarter included two cuts in the guide. So just kind of curious, with the Fed likely on pause here, you know, how we should think about that impacting the MIM and just the expansion that you've laid out.

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Justin Crowley

Okay, got it. That's helpful. And then just one last one. Just, you know, on the loans, if you could just give a little more color on the loan pipeline. You know, I know it's at record levels, but just what that, what that mix looks like and you know, what gets you confident these pull through as customers continue to get their arms around some of the uncertainty that still exists today.

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

OPERATOR

Okay, great. I appreciate it. I will leave it there. Thank you guys so much. Sure. Thanks, Jos. Your next question comes from the line of Jared Shaw from Barclays. Your line is open.

Jared Shaw

Hey guys, thanks for the question. Maybe sticking with the deposit side, you know, you have good betas through first quarter. I guess if we're in a flat rate environment with some of the expectation that, you know, competition increases. Is this sort of peak beta, do you think, here? And that could go, you know, going forward, we could see a bit of pressure.

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Yeah, I mean, the short answer is yes, I think there will be. Betas will be slower to come down than they were going up. You know, going up. Our beta was 46%.

Jared Shaw

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Jared Shaw

Yep. Okay, I appreciate that. Thanks. And then if I could just one final one. What would be, you know, as we look at second quarter with some of the moving parts on salaries and the closing of the deal, what's sort of a salary level for second quarter? And then should we expect marketing expenses to maybe trickle higher with some of this deposit initiative too?

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

So let me kind of just go down the whole list. Yeah, salary will come down. Salary will be down. Linkwater. Because of the timing of the merger as well as normal one times in the first quarter, the tackle come, tackle come down, occupancy will come down. The only thing I'm really thinking on the expense side is we were under a bit on professional. That'll probably take up a touch

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Marketing, yeah, it's fair to say that's seasonal as well. You know, home equity promotions, we've much heavier in the spring season heading into summer and yes on the deposit side. So you should see marketing tick up in Q2.

Jared Shaw

Yeah, thanks Dennis. Great, thanks. Thanks.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from a line of Damon Del Monte from kbw. Your line is open.

Damon Del Monte

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Damon Del Monte

Got it. Appreciate that. Okay, that's all that I had. Thank you.

OPERATOR

Thanks, Damon.

Lori Hunsicker

Your next question comes from the line of Lori Hunsicker from Seaport Research. Your line is open.

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Yeah, hi. Thanks. Good morning. Just wanted to go back expenses here. Can you just share with us what was the FICA expense and what was the snow removal expense this quarter? Well, let's think about it. Link quarter, FICO was 3.1 million. Link quarter, Snow was up about $650,000.

Lori Hunsicker

Link quarter. Okay, that's great. And then do you have a spot margin for March?

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Sure. 365. So up to. From the quarter. Great. Okay. Okay. And that includes basically the same amount of accretion that you reported in the quarter. I mean we talk about accretion being lumpy quarter to quarter quarter. It's even more lumpy month to month. But that's a good number.

Lori Hunsicker

Laurie, I, I know in past calls sometimes. I'll adjust it for you. This one doesn't need adjusting.

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Lori Hunsicker

Okay, great. Thanks for taking my questions.

OPERATOR

Thank you.

Matthew Breese

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

David Rosado (Chief Financial Officer)

Matthew Breese

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Matthew Breese

I'll leave it there. Thank you for taking my questions.

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Sure. Thanks, Matt.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from a line of Janet Lee from TD Bank. Your line is open.

Janet Lee

Hi, good morning.

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Morning, Jen.

Janet Lee

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

Janet Lee

Got it. For loan growth. So it looks like the loan growth will be picking up in the coming quarters. But just given the lower basis of fair to assume that it's coming in at the lower end, or do you have more optimism that it could be somewhere in the middle or even at the upper end? How should we think about the cadence of long growth picking up?

Dennis Sheehan (Chief Executive Officer)

OPERATOR

And there are no further questions at this time. I will now turn the call over to Dennis Schiem for closing remarks.

Thank you everybody for joining us. Appreciate your questions. We look forward to speaking with you at the end of our next quarter.

This concludes today's conference call. You may now disconnect.

data.title: Western Union Stock Slides After Q1 EPS Miss Despite Revenue Beat
data.description:
Western Union Company (NYSE:WU) shares are trading lower Friday after the company reported mixed first-quarter financial results.

  • Western Union shares are retreating from recent levels. Why is WU stock falling?

Q1 Highlights

Western Union reported adjusted earnings per share of 25 cents, missing the consensus estimate of 39 cents. In addition, it reported revenue of $982.70 million, beating the consensus estimate of $960.57 million.

Consumer Services revenue grew 24%, while Branded Digital revenue increased 9% and transactions rose 21% year-over-year.

The company said results were impacted by higher expenses in North America, including increased commissions tied to new agents, foreign currency impacts, lower fixed cost coverage and the absence of vendor incentive payments.

"First quarter results reflect the continued challenges in our Americas retail business as well as a few discrete items affecting the quarter," said CEO Devin McGranahan.

Western Union affirmed its fiscal-year adjusted earnings per share guidance of $1.75 to $1.85, versus the consensus estimate of $1.79. Furthermore, it affirmed its revenue guidance of $4.23 billion o $4.35 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $4.26 billion.

Western Union Shares Slip

WU Price Action: At the time of publication, Western Union shares are trading 12.97% lower at $8.12, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock
